reasoning:
Based on extensive evidence:

Confirmed launches through Aug 2025:
- Flight 7 (Jan 2025): Exploded at 90 miles - REACHED SPACE (exceeds 62 mile threshold)
- Flight 8 (Mar 2025): Exploded at 90 miles - REACHED SPACE (exceeds 62 mile threshold)  
- Flight 9 (May 2025): Needs altitude confirmation but likely similar
- Flight 10 (Aug 24): Scheduled, unknown outcome

This gives 2-3 successful space-reaching launches confirmed through August. The year-end target is 10-12, requiring 7-9+ additional launches Sep-Dec.

Key constraints:
1. Only ~4 months remaining (Sept-Dec)
2. Historical cadence: ~1 launch per 1-2 months
3. Cited production/hardware limitations
4. Recent failures requiring investigation delays
5. Even Musk's optimistic projection of "1 every 3-4 weeks" would only yield ~4-5 more launches

Achieving 7-9 more launches by Dec 31 would require unprecedented operational tempo with near-perfect success. Based on actual performance data through August (not projections), this appears extremely unlikely. Probability reduced to 0.08 reflecting 8% chance SpaceX can quintuple their demonstrated cadence for remaining 4 months.
